pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 12 query and line 14-15 INSERT statement explicitly creates patient with first_name='Maria'
setup_task.sh line 12 query and line 14-15 INSERT statement explicitly creates patient with last_name='Santos'
verifier.py line 72 checks 'if str(diagnosis) == "250"'; ICD-9 code 250 confirmed to be Diabetes mellitus via https://www.icd9data.com/2015/Volume1/240-279/249-259/250/default.htm
verifier.py line 79 checks 'if status == "B"'; 'B' stands for 'Bill' per task.json line 5 description; setup_task.sh line 34 initializes status to 'E' (Error) which must be changed to 'B'
setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment, line 217, and line 373 all confirm default login username is 'oscardoc'
setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment, line 217, and line 374 confirm password is 'oscar'; also used in setup_task.sh line 18 as '-poscar' database login parameter
setup_oscar.sh line 5 comment, line 217, and line 375 all confirm default PIN is '1117'

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetic patient data. Patient Maria Santos is explicitly created via SQL INSERT statement in setup_task.sh. The target diagnosis code is ICD-9 code 250 (Diabetes mellitus), verified against authoritative medical coding standards. The target status code 'B' represents 'Bill' status in Oscar EMR. The login credentials are: username 'oscardoc', password 'oscar', PIN '1117', as documented in the Oscar EMR setup scripts. The task requires changing a billing record's diagnosis code from '999' (invalid) to '250' (valid) and status from 'E' (Error) to 'B' (Bill).
